subject: Computer Networking
title: Antennas and Signal Strength
description: One-minute skill: Antennas and Signal Strength.

hook.text: Hold your phone differently and your signal changes. The antenna's orientation and surroundings matter enormously.

buildup.text: An antenna converts electrical signals to radio waves and vice versa. Its size is tuned to the target frequency.

discovery.text: Directional antennas focus energy one way for range. Omnidirectional ones radiate equally in all directions.

twist.text: MIMO uses multiple antennas simultaneously. More antennas means more data streams and better reliability.

climax.text: Modern routers have four or eight MIMO antennas. Your phone has at least two. Antenna arrays power modern wireless.

punchline.text: The antenna is the gateway. Better antennas mean better connections.

quiz.question: What does MIMO technology achieve?
options: Multiple simultaneous data streams using multiple antennas
Longer battery life for mobile devices
